description: Check if a date is February 29 in a leap year
constraint_code:
builder = DateSMTBuilder()
x = builder.add_date_var("x")
builder.add_constraint(x >= Date(2000, 2, 28))
builder.add_constraint(x <= Date(2000, 3, 1))
builder.add_constraint(x != Date(2000, 2, 28))
builder.add_constraint(x != Date(2000, 3, 1))
result = builder
smtlib:
; benchmark generated from python API
(set-info :status unknown)
(declare-fun x_month () Int)
(declare-fun x_year () Int)
(declare-fun x_day () Int)
(assert
 (let (($x419 (or (= x_month 4) (= x_month 6) (= x_month 9) (= x_month 11))))
 (let ((?x420 (ite $x419 30 31)))
 (let (($x412 (= (mod x_year 400) 0)))
 (let (($x405 (= (mod x_year 4) 0)))
 (let ((?x414 (ite (or (and $x405 (and (distinct (mod x_year 100) 0) true)) $x412) 29 28)))
 (let (($x403 (= x_month 2)))
 (let (($x422 (<= x_day (ite $x403 ?x414 ?x420))))
 (let (($x402 (>= x_day 1)))
 (let (($x432 (<= x_month 2)))
 (let (($x428 (>= x_month 1)))
 (let (($x431 (= x_year 2100)))
 (let (($x401 (<= x_month 12)))
 (let (($x427 (<= x_year 2099)))
 (let (($x425 (>= x_year 1901)))
 (or (and (= x_year 1900) (>= x_month 3) $x401 $x402 $x422) (and $x425 $x427 $x428 $x401 $x402 $x422) (and $x431 $x428 $x432 $x402 $x422)))))))))))))))))
(assert
 (let (($x468 (= x_year 2000)))
 (let (($x473 (and $x468 (or (> x_month 2) (and (= x_month 2) (>= x_day 28))))))
 (or (> x_year 2000) $x473))))
(assert
 (let (($x468 (= x_year 2000)))
 (let (($x487 (and $x468 (or (< x_month 3) (and (= x_month 3) (<= x_day 1))))))
 (or (< x_year 2000) $x487))))
(assert
 (not (and (= x_year 2000) (= x_month 2) (= x_day 28))))
(assert
 (not (and (= x_year 2000) (= x_month 3) (= x_day 1))))
(check-sat)

description: Adding 1 month vs adding 30 days can produce different results at month boundaries
constraint_code:
builder = DateSMTBuilder()
x = builder.add_date_var("x")
builder.add_constraint(x >= Date(2023, 1, 1))
builder.add_constraint(x <= Date(2023, 1, 31))
builder.add_constraint((x + Period(0, 1, 0)) != (x + Period(0, 0, 30)))
result = builder

description: Adding 1 year vs 366 days differs when crossing leap year boundaries
constraint_code:
builder = DateSMTBuilder()
x = builder.add_date_var("x")
builder.add_constraint(x >= Date(2019, 12, 1))
builder.add_constraint(x <= Date(2020, 2, 29))
builder.add_constraint((x + Period(1, 0, 0)) != (x + Period(0, 0, 366)))
result = builder

description: Chain of period additions that requires normalization
constraint_code:
builder = DateSMTBuilder()
x = builder.add_date_var("x")
y = builder.add_date_var("y")
builder.add_constraint(x == Date(2022, 1, 15))
builder.add_constraint(y == x + Period(0, 13, 40))
builder.add_constraint(y != Date(2023, 2, 24))
result = builder

description: Three dates must be exactly 1 month apart within a specific range
constraint_code:
builder = DateSMTBuilder()
x = builder.add_date_var("x")
y = builder.add_date_var("y")
z = builder.add_date_var("z")
builder.add_constraint(x >= Date(2022, 1, 1))
builder.add_constraint(z <= Date(2022, 5, 31))
builder.add_constraint(y == x + Period(0, 1, 0))
builder.add_constraint(z == y + Period(0, 1, 0))
builder.add_constraint(z - x == Period(0, 2, 0))
result = builder

description: End-of-month rollover from a 31-day month to a 30-day month
constraint_code:
builder = DateSMTBuilder()
x = builder.add_date_var("x")
y = builder.add_date_var("y")
builder.add_constraint(x == Date(2023, 1, 31))
builder.add_constraint(y == x + Period(0, 3, 0))
builder.add_constraint(y == Date(2023, 4, 30))
result = builder

description: Tight inequality window that is unsatisfiable
constraint_code:
builder = DateSMTBuilder()
x = builder.add_date_var("x")
builder.add_constraint(x > Date(2022, 2, 28))
builder.add_constraint(x < Date(2022, 3, 1))
result = builder
smtlib:
; benchmark generated from python API
(set-info :status unknown)
(declare-fun x_month () Int)
(declare-fun x_year () Int)
(declare-fun x_day () Int)
(assert
 (let (($x464 (or (= x_month 4) (= x_month 6) (= x_month 9) (= x_month 11))))
 (let ((?x699 (ite $x464 30 31)))
 (let (($x1032 (= (mod x_year 400) 0)))
 (let (($x635 (= (mod x_year 4) 0)))
 (let ((?x697 (ite (or (and $x635 (and (distinct (mod x_year 100) 0) true)) $x1032) 29 28)))
 (let (($x660 (= x_month 2)))
 (let (($x676 (<= x_day (ite $x660 ?x697 ?x699))))
 (let (($x1092 (>= x_day 1)))
 (let (($x626 (<= x_month 2)))
 (let (($x755 (>= x_month 1)))
 (let (($x774 (= x_year 2100)))
 (let (($x773 (<= x_month 12)))
 (let (($x772 (<= x_year 2099)))
 (let (($x878 (>= x_year 1901)))
 (or (and (= x_year 1900) (>= x_month 3) $x773 $x1092 $x676) (and $x878 $x772 $x755 $x773 $x1092 $x676) (and $x774 $x755 $x626 $x1092 $x676)))))))))))))))))
(assert
 (let (($x1186 (= x_year 2022)))
 (let (($x955 (and $x1186 (or (< x_month 2) (and (= x_month 2) (<= x_day 28))))))
 (not (or (< x_year 2022) $x955)))))
(assert
 (let (($x1186 (= x_year 2022)))
(let (($x633 (and $x1186 (or (> x_month 3) (and (= x_month 3) (>= x_day 1))))))
(not (or (> x_year 2022) $x633)))))
(check-sat)

description: Complex chain of period arithmetic with multiple variables
constraint_code:
builder = DateSMTBuilder()
p = builder.add_date_var("p")
q = builder.add_date_var("q")
x = builder.add_date_var("x")
y = builder.add_date_var("y")
builder.add_constraint(x == Date(2020, 1, 1))
builder.add_constraint(p == Period(0, 3, 15))
builder.add_constraint(q == Period(0, 1, 20))
builder.add_constraint(y == x + p + q - Period(0, 0, 5))
builder.add_constraint(y == Date(2020, 5, 30))
result = builder

description: Date transitions across leap year February boundary
constraint_code:
builder = DateSMTBuilder()
x = builder.add_date_var("x")
y = builder.add_date_var("y")
builder.add_constraint(x == Date(2020, 2, 29))
builder.add_constraint(y == x + Period(1, 0, 0))
builder.add_constraint(y != Date(2021, 2, 28))
result = builder

description: Find dates where adding 2 months equals adding 61 days
constraint_code:
builder = DateSMTBuilder()
x = builder.add_date_var("x")
builder.add_constraint(x >= Date(2023, 1, 1))
builder.add_constraint(x <= Date(2023, 12, 31))
builder.add_constraint((x + Period(0, 2, 0)) == (x + Period(0, 0, 61)))
result = builder
